content.data:
Research ONLY the repo at local path /tmp/feature-research/opencode (alias: opencode, GitHub: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode). Known for: open-source AI coding agent architecture, provider abstraction, TUI. Look especially for compaction/summarization of conversation history and any persistent notes/memory.

Research how each reference repo implements (or relates to) a "compaction-resistant notepad" for an AI coding agent.

FEATURE DEFINITION:
- Agent-writable notes with 3 priority levels: priority = permanent (must never be lost), working = session/work-in-progress, manual = manually recorded notes.
- Notes must SURVIVE context compaction (when old conversation messages get summarized/dropped to fit the context window).
- Mechanism: serialize/preserve notepad state on a PreCompact event, restore/re-inject it on a PostCompact event.
- Must integrate with the agent turn loop.
- Canonical reference is oh-my-claudecode's notepad: file-backed (.omc/notepad.md), MCP tools (notepad_read, notepad_write_priority, notepad_write_working, notepad_write_manual, notepad_prune, notepad_stats), content re-injected after compaction via system-reminder / additionalContext.

WHAT EACH SUB-AGENT MUST DO (autonomously, no off-limits files):
1. Map the assigned repo first (list files/dirs) to understand its shape. Determine the default branch via `git -C <path> rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` or the remote HEAD, and use it in all Direct Links.
2. Follow the feature signal across EVERY file/dir/language. Grep for: notepad, scratchpad, note(s), memory, persist(ent), compaction, compact, summarize, summary, context window, context limit, pre-compact, post-compact, precompact, postcompact, system-reminder, additionalContext, additional_context, inject, restore, serialize, snapshot, todo, working memory, sticky, pinned, hook.
3. Trace relevant implementations end-to-end (both callers and callees) until the full picture is clear. Read tests and config too.
4. Extract architecture patterns, API/tool surfaces, data structures, config hooks, test patterns, benchmark approaches, error handling, and extension points relevant to the feature.

RETURN EXACTLY this markdown report (keep each code snippet <=15 lines, with file:line refs):

## [repo-name] Research Report
### Relevance Score: [HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NONE]
### Why relevant: [1-2 sentences]
### Key Files
- path/to/file — [what it does re: the feature]
### Relevant Code Snippets
[short excerpts with file:line references]
### Architecture Pattern
[how this repo approaches the feature domain: notepad/memory/compaction/persistence]
### Direct Links
- https://github.com/<org>/<repo>/blob/<branch>/<file>#L<line>
### Gaps / What's Missing
[what this repo does NOT cover that the target feature needs]

If the repo has NOTHING relevant, return Relevance Score: NONE with a 1-line justification and stop. Be thorough but concise.
